evidence: No evidence a 'Superlatives' Facebook friend-tagging app was built from this pitch; only a generic Facebook 'Friends Day' superlatives feature (2016) was found, unrelated to the podcast.
TinyCo, founded 2009 in San Francisco, was already an established mobile-games-with-in-app-purchases studio (Tiny Zoo, Tiny Monsters) years before this 2019 pitch.

company: Family Guy: The Quest for Stuff (TinyCo/Fox Digital)
evidence: Licensed Family Guy mobile game shipped April 10, 2014 by TinyCo and Fox Digital Entertainment, well before the 2019 pitch.
TinyCo's licensed Marvel mobile game launched February 4, 2016, years before this 2019 pitch.
Jam City's licensed Harry Potter mobile game launched April 25, 2018, over a year before this 2019 pitch.
Native, an all-natural deodorant DTC brand founded 2015, was already acquired by P&G for ~$100M in November 2017, well before this 2019 pitch.

Documented as one of Sam Parr's real pre-Hustle side hustles (late 2000s): clickbait-titled still-image videos monetized by charging up-and-coming bands to feature their music.
Sam Parr's real college-era business selling white whiskey/moonshine online, shut down by regulators, listed among his ventures pre-dating this 2019 podcast recounting.
